lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Oh, I remember you. You're the one that discovered Lord Sinclair's murderer. Well, I can tell you, strange things are afoot once again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? What's happened?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Well, you didn't hear it from me, but I've heard the Sinclair family have vamoosed! Just up and left the mansion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean they let the murderer get away?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Of course not! Anna's safely locked in the Seers' Village jail. She'll be going to trial soon, so I've heard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anna? Is that who they arrested? I thought...hmm, never mind.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Yes, the guards received evidence from one of the family members that conclusively proved Anna did it. All other evidence was thrown out, it was that good.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: So they arrested her and soon the case will go to court.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, why is it a big deal that the Sinclairs have left, then? Maybe they wanted to get away from the memory of their father's death.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Well, it wouldn't be suspicious at all, but King Arthur has also disappeared.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: No one has seen him or the knights in quite a while. Quite a coincidence, don't you think?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How curious. Maybe I should investigate it.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: You didn't hear it from me, but I believe that guard in the garden is also investigating it. He might be able to tell you more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very odd coincidence, but I don't really have time to look into it now.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Ah, too bad. I'll have to get news from my other sources.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Have you spoken to the guard by the mansion? He won't tell me much, he called me a gossip - the nerve - but he might be willing to speak to you.

sections.title: Trying to open the large doors
Trying to break in without a permission
Talking to Pierre before talking to the guard

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, this has been bugging me for a while: Why do the Sinclairs have a dog handler on the payroll when they only have one dog?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: Have you seen that dog? It's a nasty piece of work. Even I don't like going near it.

sections.title: Talking to Stanford before talking to the guard

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: 'Allo. I don't think you'll be able to visit the mansion today, it's been locked up tight for a while now.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Good day, [player name]. I'm afraid we meet under strange circumstances again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I have heard. The town gossip informed me that something was amiss. I've come to offer my assistance.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Finally, that man serves a purpose. I'm glad to have your help. I'm baffled again and my hands are tied.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: The Sinclairs have disappeared, though I'm not sorry to see them go, but what worries me is the disappearance of King Arthur and his knights around the same time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They haven't returned to their other realm have they?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: No, there's no evidence to suggest that. It never sat well with the Sinclair children when their old man gave the castle to King Arthur and

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: moved the family to this mansion. I suspect the murder of old Sinclair was just the first step in a deeper plot to retake their 'birthright'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How goes the investigation, then? What evidence have you gathered so far?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: That's the problem. The Seers' Village council has forbidden the guards from breaking into Camelot or the Sinclair Mansion for further investigation.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: They claim it's a breach of privacy or something like that. My suspicions mean nothing without proof to back them up, but I can't get proof without losing my job.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which is why you're happy to see me, right? A rogue party could break into the mansion and investigate further, without the fear of losing their job. Am I right?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Exactly! I'm sure you'll manage to find a way into the house.

sections.title: Talking to Pierre after talking to the guard

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi. I was wondering, do you know why the mansion is all locked up?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: I'm not meant to talk to anyone outside the family. On pain of death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a bit harsh. Thanks anyway.

sections.title: Stanford after talking to the guard

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: 'Allo, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm trying to get into the mansion. Do you know why it's all locked up?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: I wasn't privy to the reason, but the whole family upped and left not long ago. Since then, I haven't heard a peep from anyone. I was told not to enter the mansion or to speak about their departure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you're willing to speak to me?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: Didn't say I was going to obey them, did I?

sections.title: Talking to Gossip after talking to the guard

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Soooooo? What did the guard have to say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Promise you won't tell?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Of course, of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The guard asked me to break into the Sinclair Mansion and search for clues about where they had gone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He said...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: that you were a gossip and I shouldn't divulge privileged information.

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: What are you doing here? I thought the house was locked up tight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, it is, except for that broken window... Very convenient.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where has the Sinclair family gone?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: I'm under strict orders not to speak to anyone not of the Sinclair family.

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: I was told if I left the house that I would be killed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think you need to look for a new job. Your employers are a bit scary.

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: What are you doing in here? I didn't think anyone could enter or leave this place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for clues as to where the Sinclair family has gone. Why can't anyone enter or leave?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: We were told a curse had been placed on us. If we left the mansion we'd be struck down in an instant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How peculiar.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Who...? Oh, I remember you! You were asking all those questions about Lord Sinclair's murder. You had all sorts of evid-whats-its.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, that was me. Now I'm trying to find out where the Sinclair family has gone.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: I don't know. They got really secretive after Lord Sinclair was killed and then suddenly left. That's all I know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: You aren't here to murder us in our sleep are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'd not be doing a very good job of it, if I was, considering you're not asleep.

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: So, you are here to kill us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, no. I'm just browsing.

sections.title: Trying to take a second scrap paper
Searching the bookcase after finding the helmet
Using any of the evidence on the servants

lines.speaker: Mary/Donovan/Louisa/Hobbes/Pierre/Stanford
lines.text: I don't know anything about that.

sections.title: Returning to the guard with the evidence

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Any luck getting into the mansion? Did you get some evidence?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have proof that the Sinclairs have left.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: A form? What does this prove?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't you see? The Sinclairs have left for good. They premeditated their departure from the mansion.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Ah! Of course. Unfortunately, they filled in all of the form except where their new address is.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Really? Where?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errr, I seem to have left it somewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have proof that links the Sinclairs to Camelot.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Aha! They've gone to the desert. They're meeting up with a camel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No...It's not camel, like the animal. The note isn't whole, they were writing the word Camelot. They were meeting at Camelot, this is our evidence to connect the Sinclairs to King Arthur.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh, right. Well, it connects them to King Arthur, but it doesn't prove that they had any bad intent towards him. For all we know they were going to a party.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have proof of foul play.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hmm, this armour resembles Black Knight armour. The only Black Knights on this side of White Wolf Mountain are those that work for Morgan Le Faye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Exactly. And we know Morgan Le Faye has a history of harassing Merlin and King Arthur.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Since there is no sign of a struggle inside the Sinclair Mansion, we can only assume the knights were there by request.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh right! With this evidence and the note you brought me, we know they had intentions to do harm to King Arthur.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]. We have all the proof we need to present to the Seers' Village council that something suspicious is going on.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: They'll review the evidence and hopefully give me permission to take a team to Camelot. It shouldn't be too difficult to get to the bottom of this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When do you expect to go to Camelot? Perhaps I could assist you?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh the Seers' Village council convenes in a month or so, I'll present it then. No doubt they'll want a few days to review the evidence.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: We'll go sometime after that...if none of the other guards are on holiday. There are only four of us, ya know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: WHAT?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A month or more? King Arthur could be in grave danger. Morgan Le Faye is involved and she is not one to be trifled with. One of the Sinclairs has already committed murder, who's to say another won't?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you're going to wait a MONTH?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I have to follow procedure, my hands are tied.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: However, were a rogue adventurer to take the matter in to their own hands...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aye, what then?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Well, if I were them, I would learn as much as I can about the Sinclair family history.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's all the advice you can give?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Well, often the best historians are the town gossips. I'd start there. Good luck, brave adventurer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

sections.title: Talking to the guard after giving him the evidence

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing again?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Find out all you can about the Sinclair family history. I'm sure there is a clue or two there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again. Can you tell me about the Sinclair family history?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Hmph. I don't see why I should tell you anything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Why not?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Perhaps if you had been less rude to me, I would have been willing to tell you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry I was rude. You are a vital part of this investigation. Unless you tell me about the Sinclairs, I won't be able to continue.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Vital part, you say? So, I'll get credit for assistance? Well...I do know quite a bit about their history. I suppose I can't hold up an investigation as important as this. What do you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the family.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: I've heard plenty about each of them...but I'll try to keep it as short as I can.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Old Lord Sinclair was a well respected man this side of White Wolf. He was great friends with King Ulthas and did many good works for the kingdom.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: His children on the other hand were spoiled and horrid. They cared for no one but themselves and gave themselves such aires. Each had their own vice, but I didn't expect any of them would be driven to murder!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What have they been up to since the arrest of their sibling?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: They've been living very quietly, a nice change from the norm. But almost too quietly. I always thought they'd get into more trouble. No good will come from them, let me tell you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the mansion.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: The mansion? The mansion is quite new. Originally, the Sinclair family lived in Camelot, although it went under a different name then.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: But when King Arthur and his knights appeared, old Lord Sinclair agreed with King Ulthas that the knights had greater need for the castle than the Sinclair family.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: So, Lord Sinclair had the mansion built, packed his family up and vacated. His children were furious with him for doing it.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: They felt themselves far better than the knights and felt insulted at having to live in a stuffy mansion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened next?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Well, you should know best... Anna killed her father. Poor man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Anna Sinclair.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Anna? The guards are keeping her in the jail in the courthouse in Seers' Village. I hear she's been acting like a royal pain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But what is she like? Can she be reasoned with?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Reasoned with? She killed her own father!

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: I hear she's being so unreasonable that no lawyer in Gielinor will represent her. She's going to get a guilty verdict for sure.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Still, I go and visit her at the Seers' Village courthouse sometimes to try and keep her spirits up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I ask you about the Sinclair family again?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Well, I'm not one to gossip, but as it's for your investigation... What would you like to know?

sections.title: Talking to the guard after learning about Anna

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is Anna Sinclair being held?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: At the Seers' Village courthouse.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about Anna Sinclair?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: What? I'm no gossip; I've got nothing to say about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about Anna?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: Why do you want to know about her?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I may need her help to get in touch with the rest of the Sinclairs.

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: Good luck, mate. She's a right pain in the...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In the...?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: Sorry, you still here? I started thinking about turnips.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about Anna?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: Anna? She was a spoiled brat, that's for sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't you tell me anything more useful than that?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: I'm afraid not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about Anna Sinclair?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: Anna? Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Because she may be responsible for murder!

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: Oh yes. Well, I can't say I ever liked Anna. She was always chasing after some boy or other.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about Anna?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Anna? I don't know very much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You must have been privy to some of the children's secrets. Anything would be helpful.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: No, I don't know anything like that. I kept my head down and did my work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about Anna Sinclair?

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: Anna?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Anna.

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: Well, she's a murderer. What more could you need to know?

sections.title: Talking to guard 1 at the courthouse

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: That Sinclair woman is such a pain! She keeps demanding gourmet meals and ridiculous things like silk sheets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak to her?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Go ahead. It means she won't be demanding things from me.

sections.title: Talking to guard 2 at the courthouse

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Mother said to join the guards, see the world! What do I end up doing? Guarding some stuck-up brat in a backwater village full of hippies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: May I speak to the prisoner?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I don't see why not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Do you remember me?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Hmmm...yes, you were that nosy person asking all those questions about daddy's murder.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So he's 'daddy' now is he? If he was so dear to you why did you kill him?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: I didn't! No one will believe me. It was my brother. He set me up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But there's hard evidence against you.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: It's forged! I swear, I never touched that dagger or poisoned my father.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't come here to just chit-chat. I need to know where your family has disappeared to. I have suspicions they've reclaimed Camelot.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: How should I know? I've been locked up here since you stuck your nose into our family's business.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'll be off then. Have fun spending the rest of your life in jail.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Wait!

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: I... I might know how to find them...but I need a favour from you in return.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Name your price.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: I need a defence lawyer. None of the ones around here will represent me, they all think I'm guilty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe it's because you ARE guilty. Your sibling offered hard proof you did it!

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: No he didn't! He just said he saw me in the room. Why does everyone believe him?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Can't you see he framed me? I was the scapegoat so they could all inherit their fortunes and live the good life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why should I believe you? As far as I know you did kill your father.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: I don't care if you don't believe me. I know I'm innocent...but I'm also the only one that can show you how to get into Camelot. There's a secret passage that I discovered as a child when we lived there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, if I agree to get you a 'not guilty' verdict at trial, you promise to tell me how to get into this secret passage that will lead me straight into Camelot?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Yes! Exactly. So, what do you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I guess I don't have much of a choice. I'll go start gathering evidence.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Try speaking to the servants at the Mansion. They'll probably be able to give you useful testimony.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Wait, take this.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: You can use it as evidence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of what, your guilt?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: No, show it to the servants. You'll find one of my siblings wears similar colours.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, thank you.

sections.title: Talking to Anna after agreeing to help her

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Have you made any progress?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working on it. It's not exactly easy to unprove something that's true.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Try speaking to the servants at the Mansion. They'll probably be able to give you useful testimony.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Have you made any progress?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need a piece of thread from the clothes you wore the night of the murder.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Whatever for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I might need to show it to people when I ask them questions about the murder. It's evidence in the case.

sections.title: Talking to Gossip after becoming an attorney

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: I hear you're the defence lawyer for Anna, now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can you possibly know that already?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: I'm the town gossip, I'm supposed to know this stuff. Are you preparing for the trial?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm going to talk to the servants and see if I can learn anything new from them...but other than that, I don't really know what I'm doing.

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Well, my father was in the guards and he attended many trials. As a defence lawyer you need to disprove any evidence presented by the prosecution. Disproving it gives reasonable doubt as to whether or not your

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: client actually committed the crime.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But how do I know what evidence will be presented?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Some of it will be the evidence you found: the dagger, the poison and the thread. There was also an eyewitness saying they saw Anna tamper with Lord Sinclair's dinner. Start asking questions about those things and

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: see where you get.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm representing Anna Sinclair as a defence lawyer.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Ha, ha, ha. That's a good one. Whoo, I needed a laugh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, I'm not joking. I have to prove she is innocent.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: WHAT?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: But the proof against her is conclusive. Someone saw her tamper with Sinclair's dinner!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: One of her siblings claims to have seen that, but who can say if they're telling the truth?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Besides, it's for King Arthur. Anna is the only lead I have on how to find the King and his knights.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: This is a sad, sad day. I can't stop you from doing it, but I wish with all my heart you wouldn't. You'll be aiding a murderer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm representing Anna Sinclair as her defence lawyer. I need to ask you some questions about what happened the night of Lord Sinclair's murder.

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: Dang, that's too bad for you. Isn't it a bit of conflict of interest, considering you were part of the investigation that proved she was guilty?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, anyways, I need to ask you some questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the night of the murder?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: I fed the dog and went to the inn in town. When I got back the guards were all over the place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you last see Anna Sinclair on that fateful night?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: Didn't see any of the family. I was outdoors most of the evening, tending to things, then went to town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you ever see Anna in possession of the dagger that was found embedded in Lord Sinclair's body?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: That dagger? I think Lord Sinclair kept it in the library. It was only decorative, so never paid much attention to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, any of the family or servants would have had access to it.

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: Yes, I suppose they would.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, I seem to have forgotten the thread I was going to show you. I'll need to get it from Anna Sinclair at the jail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me which Sinclair family member this thread belongs to?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: I'm not sure; it could be Anna's or David's.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, you can't identify the owner of the thread for certain?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: I can't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the poison?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: The poison salesman came around the mansion and practically everyone bought some.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me exactly who bought poison from him?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: Each of the Sinclair kids did. They all had chores they were meant to do. I had a chat with that salesman. Whoo, you any idea how strong that stuff was? Just a small amount would be enough to kill a cow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, you're completely certain all of the Sinclair children bought poison? And it would only have needed a small portion to kill Lord Sinclair.

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: Yup, that's what the poison salesman said. I had a chat with him at the pub.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That means someone could have used a small amount to frame Anna and still have done their chore. That's great news.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm representing Anna Sinclair as her defence lawyer. I need to ask you some questions about what happened the night of Lord Sinclair's murder.

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: Why in Gielinor would you want to do that? That's a lost cause if I ever heard one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know, I know, but circumstances... Well, I just need to ask you some questions, alright?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the night of the murder?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: Not all that much. I worked out here 'til late. I was getting ready to come in for dinner when all the commotion started.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you last see Anna Sinclair on that fateful night?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: She must have been in the house, since I didn't see her out here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Didn't you hear the glass breaking when the window was smashed by the murderer?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: No, I was on this side of the garden and you can't hear much but the dog from over here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Was the dog barking that night?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you ever see Anna in possession of the dagger that was found embedded in Lord Sinclair's body?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: Hmm, not that I can remember. It was kept on display as some sort of replica of a great weapon. I'm not much good at history so I can't remember any details.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you see anyone else handle the dagger?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: I think the maid may have dusted it, but I don't really know, I work outside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me which Sinclair family member this thread belongs to?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: I'm not sure; it could be Anna's or David's.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, you can't identify the owner of the thread for certain?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the poison?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: This salesman came around selling this poison that could fix anything. I think most everyone bought some to use for chores around the mansion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you buy any?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: No, but I requested one of the Sinclairs buy some to use on the compost. I can't remember if they did or not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did Anna Sinclair buy any poison?

lines.speaker: Stanford
lines.text: Yes, she did. I definitely remember seeing her buying some. I'm pretty sure she didn't use it for her chore either.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear. That's not good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm representing Anna Sinclair as her defence lawyer. I need to ask you some questions about what happened the night of Lord Sinclair's murder.

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: Didn't you already ask me questions about this? You were investigating the case and got all that evidence. Besides, Anna was proven guilty - by one of her own siblings.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errr, well, circumstances have changed. Anyway, I need to ask you some questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the night of the murder?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: I was tidying the bedrooms most of the afternoon and then assisted Louisa in the kitchen, preparing dinner. I gave Lord Sinclair's dinner to Mary, and shortly there after heard her screaming.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you last see Anna Sinclair on that fateful night?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: I'm afraid I didn't see her. I didn't meet anyone when I walked from the bedrooms to the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you ever see Anna in possession of the dagger that was found embedded in Lord Sinclair's body?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: No, I only saw Lord Sinclair handle it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No one else ever touched it?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: Well, now that you have me thinking about it, I believe I saw David handle it once. I'm not really sure what he was doing with it, but I do remember him looking at it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's wonderful? Why didn't you mention it before? It means it could have been planted to make it seem like Anna did it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me which Sinclair family member this thread belongs to?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: I'm not sure; it could be Anna's or David's.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, you can't identify the owner of the thread, for certain?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: I'm afraid not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the poison?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: There were various chores around the house that required poison.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you remember who bought poison?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: I personally did not; I do not know about the others.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm representing Anna Sinclair as her defence lawyer. I need to ask you some questions about what happened the night of Lord Sinclair's murder.

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: About the murder? Well, there's not much to tell, honestly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry to trouble you, but I really need some answers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the night of the murder?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: I was with Hobbes the butler and Mary the maid in the kitchen. We were preparing dinner. I gave Mary Lord Sinclair's meal and sent her on her way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, you didn't see Mary deliver the food to Lord Sinclair?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: Of course not. I was here cooking, she was taking the meal to him. That's when she found the body.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then someone could have tampered with the meal while she was on the way?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: I can't imagine how. Mary carries the meal right in front of her the entire way!

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: But now that you mention it, I do remember seeing Anna Sinclair hanging about the kitchen earlier that day. She looked a bit shifty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, right. That doesn't bode well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you last see Anna Sinclair on that fateful night?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: I was in the kitchen almost all day, but I did see her just before I began cooking the evening meal. She was wandering aimlessly. So, I snapped at her to get out of the way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did she do then?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: Stuck her tongue out at me and wandered away. Useless girl.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's not very conclusive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you ever see Anna in possession of the dagger that was found embedded in Lord Sinclair's body?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: Not that I can remember. None of the children seemed to pay any attention to it, as it was just a replica and not worth anything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you see anyone else in possession of it?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: I suppose Mary dusted it now and then, but I think it just sat on one of the bookshelves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me which Sinclair family member this thread belongs to?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: It could be Anna's or David's.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, you can't identify the owner of the thread, for certain?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: I'm afraid not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the poison?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: I told Carol to buy some for the drains. I haven't a clue if she actually did or not, she rarely paid me any heed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who else bought poison?

lines.speaker: Louisa
lines.text: I don't know. The Sinclairs were so stingy about money that I'm surprised any of them bought some.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm representing Anna Sinclair as her defence lawyer. I need to ask you some questions about what happened the night of Lord Sinclair's murder.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: I don't like talking about that night. I still have nightmares about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry to put you through it again, but I really have to get some answers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the night of the murder?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: What an awful night. It started out normally, I was in the kitchen with Hobbes and Louisa, helping with dinner. They gave me Lord Sinclair's meal. I went to deliver it and I found him...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There, there. I don't need you to tell me any more of that. What about on the way to the study? Did you see Anna?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: No, I didn't pass Anna, I'm sure of that. I think I passed one of the other young Sinclairs though. A man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? So, you can't place Anna at the murder scene, but there was a man there? David, Bob or Frank?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: I can't remember. I just remember it being a man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's great news.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you last see Anna Sinclair on that fateful night?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: I was cleaning the library. I saw her come into the room.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was she doing?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Nothing really, just wandering.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She wasn't acting suspicious?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: No, nothing like that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you see her anywhere near the scene of the murder?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: No, I never did. And I walked from the kitchen to Lord Sinclair's room, so I would have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very interesting. That's very helpful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you ever see Anna in possession of the dagger that was found embedded in Lord Sinclair's body?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Not that I can think of.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Not really. I can't remember who handled it, if anyone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me which Sinclair family member this thread belongs to?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Hmm, it could be Anna's or David's.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, you can't identify the owner of the thread for certain?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: I can't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the poison?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Nothing, really. I didn't buy any.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know who did?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: No, I was upstairs cleaning when the salesman came by, so I didn't know until after he left that he'd been here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm representing Anna Sinclair as her defence lawyer. I need to ask you some questions about what happened the night of Lord Sinclair's murder.

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: But she was proven guilty! There was even evidence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I have my doubts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the night of the murder?

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: I went to bed early as I'd been up very early fixing some garden equipment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, you didn't see or hear anything unusual?

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: No, I'm a very heavy sleeper. I didn't even hear the maid scream when she found the body. Louisa, the cook, had to come wake me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you last see Anna Sinclair on that fateful night?

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: Let's see. I'd finished up early for the day and was heading to bed. I can't remember seeing any one on my way there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you certain?

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: Pretty certain. I usually tried to avoid being near any of the Sinclairs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you ever see Anna in possession of the dagger that was found embedded in Lord Sinclair's body?

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: Hmm, I seem to remember her looking at it a few days before the murder.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? That's not good. Not good at all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me which Sinclair family member this thread belongs to?

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: I'm not sure; it could be Anna's or David's.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, you can't identify the owner of the thread for certain?

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: I can't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the poison?

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: Nothing much. I didn't buy any and I don't remember who did.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're certain you don't remember anything?

lines.speaker: Donovan the Family Handyman
lines.text: Yup, I don't pay heed to salesmen, they're a dodgy lot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, I think I'll gather more evidence, first.

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: This court is now in session.

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: This trial is to determine the guilt or innocence of Anna Sinclair for the murder of Lord Sinclair.

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: Is the prosecution ready?

lines.speaker: Prosecutor
lines.text: The prosecution is ready, Your Honour.

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: Is the defence ready?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The defence is ready, Your Honour.

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: Very well. The prosecution may present its case.

lines.speaker: Prosecutor
lines.text: Thank you, Your Honour.

lines.speaker: Prosecutor
lines.text: Members of the jury, I am going to present you with evidence that decisively proves Anna Sinclair is a murderer.

lines.speaker: Prosecutor
lines.text: First, the dagger. The dagger was found stabbed into Lord Sinclair's body; it has the fingerprints of Anna Sinclair all over it.

lines.speaker: Prosecutor
lines.text: Second, the thread. This thread was found at the scene of the crime; it matches Anna Sinclair's clothes.

lines.speaker: Prosecutor
lines.text: Last, but certainly not least, the poison that actually killed Lord Sinclair; it was found at the scene of the crime and had been bought by the defendant. Members of the jury, this woman is guilty.

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: Thank you, prosecutor.

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: Defence, as this is your first time in court, I'm going to explain how we do things around here.

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: You may call for a recess at any time by leaving through the gate behind you. You'll have to start the trial over when you return. You may call witnesses by speaking to me. When you are finished with a witness

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: simply speak to me and call the next one.

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: Your job is to disprove the evidence presented by the prosecution by having your witnesses testify. If you need to hear the prosecution's case again, just speak to him. Let's begin.

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: Who would you like to call as a witness?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The defence calls the Sinclairs' handyman.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The defence calls the Sinclairs' dog handler.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The defence calls the Sinclairs' butler.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The defence calls the Sinclairs' maid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your name and occupation?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: Hobbes; I am the butler.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you ever see Anna in possession of the dagger that was found embedded in Lord Sinclair's body?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: No, I only saw Lord Sinclair handle it. It was on display in the library.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No one else ever touched it?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: Well, now that you have me thinking about it, I believe I saw Frank handle it once. I'm not really sure what he was doing with it, but I do remember him looking at it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You are saying that you saw someone other than Anna handle the dagger?

lines.speaker: Hobbes
lines.text: Yes, I am.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not only has a person other than my client been identified handling the weapon, but the dagger was within easy access to anyone in the household.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My client's fingerprints could have been planted on it by another party.

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: Order! ORDER!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your name and occupation?

lines.speaker: Donovan
lines.text: Donovan; I'm the handyman.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell us who this thread belongs to?

lines.speaker: Donovan
lines.text: That thread could be Anna's or David's; they always seemed to be wearing that colour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, you can't say for certain who this thread belongs to.

lines.speaker: Donovan
lines.text: No, sorry, I can't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then this thread could have come from either of Anna or David's clothes, and they had many of this colour. A fact that would be known to anyone of the household.

lines.speaker: Donovan
lines.text: Sure, anybody would have seen that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then the thread could have been planted by someone else to throw suspicion on my client. I think we can all see this thread is very questionable evidence.

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: Order! ORDER!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your name and occupation?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: Pierre; I'm the dog handler.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the poison?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: The poison salesman came around the mansion and practically everyone bought some.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me exactly who bought poison from him?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: Each of the Sinclair kids did; they all had chores they were meant to do. I had a chat with that salesman. Whoo-boy, you any idea how strong that stuff was? Just a small amount would be enough to kill a cow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, only a small amount of poison would be enough to kill a man, I suppose?

lines.speaker: Pierre
lines.text: Yeah, I s'pose it would.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Therefore, any one of the Sinclairs who bought poison could have done their chore and poisoned Lord Sinclair as well. Just because my client was too lazy to do her chore doesn't mean she poisoned Lord Sinclair.

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: Order! ORDER!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The defence calls the Sinclairs' maid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your name and occupation?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Mary; I'm the maid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you last see Anna on that fateful night?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: I was in the kitchen with Hobbes and Louisa, helping with dinner. They gave me Lord Sinclair's meal. I went to deliver it and I found him...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about on the way to the study? Did you see Anna?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: No, I didn't pass Anna, I'm sure of that. I think I passed one of the other young Sinclairs, though. A man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? So, you can't place Anna at the murder scene, but there was a man there? David, Bob or Frank?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: I can't remember. I just remember it being a man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed. Well, if my client can't be placed at the murder scene, and there is evidence that a man was there, I think it casts a great deal of doubt on the case.

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: Order! ORDER!

sections.title: After successfully disproving all four evidences

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: I believe we've heard enough.

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: Would the jury please pronounce their verdict?

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: Very well. Anna Sinclair, you are hereby declared 'not guilty' by a jury of your peers. You are free to go.

sections.title: Talking to Anna after winning the case

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: I can't believe you did it. I'm free!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but what about our deal?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Shhh. Don't talk about that here! The guards are going to take me back to my cell for paperwork. Meet me there and I'll explain how to get into Camelot.

sections.title: Talking to Prosecutor after the case is over

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's with the balloons?

lines.speaker: Prosecutor
lines.text: Ah, the court house was built over the Party Room...

lines.speaker: Prosecutor
lines.text: Balloons we can handle, it's when those dancing knights show up that it gets troublesome.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, being an attorney was an interesting experience.

lines.speaker: Prosecutor
lines.text: Those were some dirty tricks you played. Your so- called 'proof' doesn't hold up under any scrutiny.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know, but I needed to get a 'not guilty' verdict.

lines.speaker: Prosecutor
lines.text: You've let a cold-blooded murderer go free. I hope the guilt eats away at you.

sections.title: Talking to Judge after the case is over

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's with the balloons?

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: Ah, the court house was built over the Party Room...

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: Balloons we can handle, it's when those dancing knights show up that it gets troublesome.

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: That was a well presented case. Court is now over, so you may leave when you like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak to my client?

lines.speaker: The judge
lines.text: Of course. You can speak to them here or back at the jail cell. They'll be released later today after some paperwork gets filled out.

sections.title: Talking to Anna in her cell

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: I can't believe such an incompetent fool as you managed to prove me innocent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You need to hold up your end of the bargain. Tell me how to get into Camelot or I go back to the judge and tell him I have doubts about your innocence.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: You are demanding for a peasant, aren't you. Walk around the edge of Camelot towards the back of the castle. From the hedges, you should be able to see the garden temple. There's a statue back there, if you pull

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: the arm a secret entrance will appear. Go inside and it will take you straight into Camelot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you going to be doing? Aren't you coming with me?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: I have to sign some paper work before I can leave here. Besides, the last thing I want to do is go hang out with my family. I'm sure you can manage this on your own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Riiight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get into Camelot again?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Walk around the edge of Camelot towards the back of the castle. From the hedges, you should be able to see the garden temple. There's a statue back there, if you pull the arm a secret entrance will appear. Go inside

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: and it will take you straight into Camelot.

sections.title: Searching the statue near Camelot castle

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: You see, I told you that adventurer would turn up. He believed my every word.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Well done, sibling. I knew you'd manage to get a 'not guilty' verdict, but to lure this cretin here as well...

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: What can I say, I'm impressed.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Why thank you. What are we going to do with him now?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Enough! I'm sick of having to listen to your drivel.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Do you think 'she' would want to see him?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Most definitely. Have someone go and fetch her.

lines.speaker: Morgan Le Faye
lines.text: I hear you have something of interest to me.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Yes, my lady. The meddling adventurer who helped King Arthur in the past.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I knew it. I knew Morgan Le Faye had to be involved. Where is King Arthur and his knights? What have you done with them?

lines.speaker: Morgan Le Faye
lines.text: Oh, they are quite safe. You'll be seeing them soon, although not Arthur. He's a bit...busy at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll foil your plans Morgan, just like I always do. I won't let you win. Good alwa-

lines.speaker: Morgan Le Faye
lines.text: I'm tired of this.

sections.title: Trying to open the cell door

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should speak to Merlin first.

sections.title: Talking to the knights before talking to Merlin

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you let a bunch of Black Knights get the better of you? I thought you guys were the toughest knights around.

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: We are true knights who fight for honour. Those Black Knights didn't fight fair, so we were unprepared. Most unchivalrous of them.

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: Isn't it terrible what happened to King Arthur? He was turned to stone before my eyes! I have failed him, I am not worthy of being his knight. To allow such lowlifes to take Camelot right from under our noses... It's unforgivable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Buck up, man. We'll escape from here and set everything to right.

lines.speaker: Sir Kay
lines.text: I will wreak havoc upon those worthless Sinclairs. Betraying their father's wishes like that, it's despicable.

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: I could have taken all of them on! Those Black Knights wouldn't stand a chance against me, the greatest knight in the land.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then why are you in this jail?

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: They attacked while I was in the bath. What was I to do?

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: Hey, are you laughing at me, the greatest knight in the land?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh, oh no. I wouldn't laugh at the greatest knight in the land.

lines.speaker: Sir Lucan
lines.text: Please, don't ask me any questions. I am still too upset from our defeat at the hands of those treacherous Sinclairs.

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: Greetings, brave knight. I believe you should speak to Merlin, he probably has need of your assistance.

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Oh dear, did Morgan capture you too?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, no. It was all part of my brilliant plan. I allowed her to capture me so I could find out where she was keeping all of you.

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Eh, is that so? Very clever. Now that you've found us, what is your plan, oh brilliant leader?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, why do I have to do all the thinking here? You're the brilliant wizard.

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Well, all great plans start with knowledge. Let me bring you up to speed with the situation. What do you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is King Arthur now? He's not in the jail cell with us.

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: As the final blow to our morale, Morgan turned Arthur into a granite statue before our very eyes. He was taken away by the Black Knights, for what purposes I know not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is the connection between Morgan Le Faye and the Sinclairs?

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Morgan Le Faye has been itching to get revenge upon Arthur and myself for some time. When she heard of the murder of old Sinclair, she learned she was not the only one with a vendetta against Camelot.

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: The Sinclairs have wanted to get their slimy hands on Camelot since their father's death. With Morgan's cunning and their riches, they make a formidable team. They bought the services of the Black Knights from

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Lord Daquarius and overran Camelot. We were outnumbered and taken by surprise.

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: The Sinclairs stole our home, but Morgan struck the greater blow by stealing the Holy Grail, Arthur's most prized possession.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's the plan of escape? Are you going to teleport us all away? Turn us into fish and swim to sea?

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: During the battle at Camelot, Morgan cast a spell against me which appears to have stripped me of my powers. I must admit, the loss of my magic has left me befuddled, making it difficult to create a plan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm sure if we put our heads together we will think of something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about a plan of escape.

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Escape is only a short-term plan. We should discuss the long-term goals as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How will we go about reclaiming Camelot?

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: I think I could reclaim Camelot from the Sinclairs on my own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you plan to do that? You couldn't even defend it from them.

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: They don't know I've lost my magic. Besides, I've got a few tricks up my sleeves. For all their snobberies, they're still just spoiled brats. It shouldn't be difficult, but time is of the essence. I need you to break me out

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: of here quickly and quietly, so Morgan cannot alert them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So to reclaim Camelot, I need to break you out of jail quietly.

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Precisely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I retrieve the Holy Grail? Do you know where it is?

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Morgan always keeps things of power near her. So the Grail will be somewhere in Keep Le Faye. However, it will most likely be disguised and booby-trapped. Morgan has a thing about transmogrifying.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The last time I was in Keep Le Faye, it was pretty well guarded. I don't think I'll be able to sneak in quietly.

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: No, that won't work. Better to break out of the jail with all of the Round Table Knights. Let them deal with the guards while you retrieve the Grail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, I need to break eight knights out of jail? That could be troublesome.

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Often the most complicated things have the simplest answers. Try leaving through the door.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But it's locked.

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: When has that ever stopped an intrepid adventurer such as yourself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Assuming I can escape from this jail, how can I save King Arthur?

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: I believe he's been taken to the headquarters of the Black Knights. I'm afraid I'm not well travelled on that side of the world, so I don't know its exact location. However, it's trickier than just finding Arthur. You'll

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: need to free him of the spell that turned him into a granite statue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how do I go about doing that?

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: I have no idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: WHAT?

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Let me finish. I may have no idea, but a wizard friend of mine should. His name is Wizard Cromperty, and he's very experienced in freeing people from stone since he keeps teleporting them into it. Speak to him in East

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Ardougne, he'll help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

sections.title: Talking to the knights after talking to Merlin

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any thoughts on how we could escape?

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: No, but when we do, I'm going to make those Black Knights black and blue all over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you had any brilliant ideas on how to get out of here?

lines.speaker: Sir Palomedes
lines.text: You usually leave rooms through doors, but this one is locked.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any thoughts on how we can get out of this cell?

lines.speaker: Sir Kay
lines.text: I saw a grate high up on the wall of the cell. I think someone could slip through that, but I'm not sure how they would reach it.

lines.speaker: Sir Tristram
lines.text: I have great faith that you will lead us to victory. But first we must win the battle, then worry about the war. If you require my assistance in escaping from this wretched cell, please tell me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, I will let you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas...

lines.speaker: Sir Lucan
lines.text: Please, no questions. I am in mourning for our King.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any thoughts on how we could break out of this cell?

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: Well, I would have thought picking the lock was really the best way, but I don't have the knowledge or tools to do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas on how to get out of this cell?

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: Sir Kay and I noticed a grate high up on the wall that leads to the small docks at the back of the keep, but it's too high up to reach and we couldn't figure out a way to boost our height.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We don't have a ladder...but we do have a lot of knights.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey guys! I've got an idea...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey guys, do you think you could lift me out too? I need to run some errands. I'll come back and free you all as soon as I can!

sections.title: Trying to open the Camelot castle gate, Sinclair mansion gate, Keep La Faye door or search the statue
Talking to the knights after rescuing Merlin

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: How are you going to get all of us out of here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure yet. Have you got any useful objects in your pockets?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pie? How very useful to our cause.

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: How are you going to get all of us out of here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We need to get through the cell door, but I haven't got anything to open it. Have you got any useful objects in your pockets?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, these will be a huge help.

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: How are you going to get all of us out of here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure yet. Have you got any useful objects in your pockets?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gee, thanks. You've contributed so much to my plan.

lines.speaker: Sir Palomedes
lines.text: How are you going to get all of us out of here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working on it. Have you got any useful objects in your pockets?

lines.speaker: Sir Palomedes
lines.text: I'm sorry, I don't have any pockets.

lines.speaker: Sir Lucan
lines.text: How are you going to get all of us out of here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure yet. Have you got any useful objects in your pockets?

lines.speaker: Sir Lucan
lines.text: I'm afraid not. I didn't have anything with me when we were captured.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for inspiration. Have you got any useful objects in your pockets?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks... I think.

lines.speaker: Sir Kay
lines.text: How are you going to get all of us out of here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The quickest way is through the cell door, but I don't have anything to pick the lock with. Have you got any useful objects in your pockets?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, thanks.

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: How are you going to get all of us out of here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure yet. Have you got any useful objects in your pockets?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

sections.title: Trying to open the doors without a lockpick
Attempting to go down the ladder

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Need a hand?

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: No, my revenge will be my own. You must retrieve the Holy Grail!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: WHOO! Get 'em! Show no mercy.

lines.speaker: Sir Kay
lines.text: They shall pay for what they have done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Give me an R. Give me an O. Give me a U...

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: In the amount of time it will take you to spell out Round Table Knights, you could have found the Grail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Give him a right hook!

lines.speaker: Sir Lucan
lines.text: How would I do that when I'm holding a sword? Leave me to the fighting, you find the Holy Grail!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You guys deal with the guards, I'll get the Holy Grail

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: Consider it done.

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: DIE SCUM!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How's it going, Sir Lancelot?

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: How dare you attempt to imprison the greatest knight in the land? Have at thee!

lines.speaker: Renegade Knight
lines.text: Please! Stop the hurting!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Show them what for!

lines.speaker: Sir Tristram
lines.text: What for what?

sections.title: Talking to the knights in the cell after an unsuccessful escape

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: I'll make them pay for what they've done to King Arhtur.

lines.speaker: Sir Palomedes
lines.text: I fear we may never escape this dank dungeon.

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: How could this have happened to us?

lines.speaker: Sir Lucan
lines.text: How could this have happened to us?

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: Sad is the day when the Round Table Knights fail their King.

lines.speaker: Sir Kay
lines.text: I shall avenge Arthur for this misdeed!

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: Let me out! Let me out, I say!

sections.title: Solving the riddle and finding the grail

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now I just need to get out of here and see Wizard Cromperty.

sections.title: Opening the grail box for another grail

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Hello there. My name is Cromperty. I am a wizard, and an inventor.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: You must be [player name]. My good friend Sedridor has told me about you. As both wizard and inventor, he has aided me in my great invention!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wizard Cromperty! Merlin sent me. King Arthur has been kidnapped and turned into a granite statue.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Why would he want to be a statue?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He didn't WANT to be a statue. Morgan Le Faye turned him into one.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Oh yes, I see now. Why are you telling me all this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Merlin said you might know how to free King Arthur.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Oh, well, I don't know anything about rocks. I'm a teleportation expert, myself. But there was this one time I teleported a girl right into a rock. What a mess that was.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Wizard Cromperty, I remember, I was there.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Oh were you? Of course you were. Well, I suppose being turned into a statue is very similar to being teleported into a rock. Let me see what we'll need.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: If my calculations are correct, this spell will need a bit more 'oomph' than last time around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is that?

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Well, before you were simply reanimating the person within the rock. This time you need to unravel Morgan Le Faye's spell first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I do that?

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: You'll need King Arthur's most prized possession. That will unravel Morgan's spell. Then you'll need a piece of granite, the same rock Arthur was turned into.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not so difficult. I already have the Holy Grail, King Arthur's most prized possession, and I know he was turned into a granite statue.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Let me just get that spell scroll for you...

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Oh, it looks like you still have the scroll I gave you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do I do with this spell?

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Take the scroll, the Holy Grail and a piece of granite, any amount will do, to the statue of King Arthur. Once in front of him, read the scroll and the magic should do the rest.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Where is King Arthur being kept, anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Merlin suspects King Arthur had been taken to the Black Knights' Fortress. The last time I was there I needed to disguise myself in a bronze medium helm and an iron chainbody just to get past the door.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Well, that should still get you into the fortress, but I imagine you'll need a better disguise than that to reach King Arthur. He's a very important person and will be well guarded by the Black Knights.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Don't underestimate them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, Wizard Cromperty!

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Hmm...I wonder...if you used a magical object that turned you into stone on the statue, would it turn him back to being human? I must research that...

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Oh, are you still here? Go on then, you've got a king to save!

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Hello there. My name is Cromperty. I am a wizard, and an inventor.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: You must be [player name]. My good friend Sedridor has told me about you. As both wizard and inventor, he has aided me in my great invention!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Wizard Cromperty.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Why hello, [player name], what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: There you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did I need for the spell to work?

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Take the scroll, the Holy Grail and a piece of granite, of any size, shape or weight, to the statue of King Arthur. Once in front of him, read the scroll and the magic should do the rest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get into the Black Knights' Fortress?

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: How should I know, I'm just a simple wizard.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: However, from what you said about having to wear a helm and chainmail, it sounds like you were disguised as a mere guard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And...

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Well, King Arthur's statue wouldn't be trusted to mere guards. Only fully-fledged Black Knights will guard it. So you'll need to disguise yourself as one of them if you want to get access to the statue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course! Thanks, Wizard Cromperty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

sections.title: Infiltrating the Black Knights' Fortress... again

lines.speaker: Fortress Guard
lines.text: I heard something happened to King Arthur and that Lord Daquarius was behind it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? Where is King Arthur now?

lines.speaker: Fortress Guard
lines.text: I have no idea, but it's a great victory for Lord Daquarius. To take such a mighty supporter of the White Knights down... Truly brilliant.

lines.speaker: Fortress Guard
lines.text: Good day, brave knight. Congratulations on your victory against King Arthur.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errr, thanks. Why don't you tell me in your own words how glorious you thought our victory was.

lines.speaker: Fortress Guard
lines.text: Of course. It was so clever of Lord Daquarius to lend our men to Morgan Le Faye in exchange for our prize. We got the better end of the deal, no doubt.

sections.title: Trying to free King Arthur without all the items

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have everything necessary to cast the spell.

sections.title: Talking to King Arthur without a guard disguise

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to bring you some guard armour to disguise yourself in or you'll never be able to escape.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Very well. I'll await you here.

sections.title: Talking to King Arthur with a spare disguise

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Thank you! I was afraid that would be the end of me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's no trouble. But we need to get you out of here quickly before any of the Black Knights suspect something.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: The Black Knights? I thought Morgan did this to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's no time to explain; here, take this disguise.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: You leave first and I'll sneak out in a few minutes. Meet me back at Camelot and I shall reward you generously for your aid.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: You leave first and I'll sneak out in a few minutes. Meet me back at Camelot and I shall reward you generously for your aid.

sections.title: Trying to climb down ladder after rescuing Arthur

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should go meet King Arthur at Camelot. He should have escaped by now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see you made it back safely.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Indeed, thanks to the disguise you gave me. I arrived back just as Merlin had kicked out the Sinclair children. He scared them off with a few puffs of smoke and frogs. He told them if any of them left their house

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: again, he'd turn them all into rabbits.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess we won't be hearing a peep out of them for awhile.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Indeed. We of the Round Table are greatly indebted to you for all of your help. I wish to reward you for all the help you have given us...

sections.title: Talking to Gossip before finishing the quest

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Do you have any interesting news?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, no?

lines.speaker: Gossip
lines.text: Then what are you standing around for!

sections.title: Talking to Sinclair guard before turning in the quest

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I'm afraid I'm too busy to speak to you right now.

sections.title: Talking to King Arthur after the quest

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Welcome, brave knight. I am always happy to see you back at my court. Please feel free to roam the castle and make use of the training grounds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What training grounds?

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: My knights train daily on the top floor of Camelot. There they test their skills and strength against each other. Because of all the help you have given us, I open this training ground to you as well.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Speak to the squire on the top floor of Camelot - he will explain the rules of the training grounds in detail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how did you remove the Sinclairs from Camelot?

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Well, after I climbed out the vent, I snuck aboard a passing trade ship that dropped me at Catherby. From there, I returned to Camelot and used the Sinclairs own secret entrance against them. I used some of my

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: own inventions to create a great deal of chaos and drove them from the house. Then I made sure they returned to their mansion and told them if they ever stepped foot from it again, I'd turn them all into rabbits.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you get your magic powers back?

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Ah, Morgan's spell was not hard to break once I had access to my workshop. It's rather technical, so I won't bore you with the details.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You had an exciting day!

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Greetings, brave knight!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. What's through that door?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: That door leads to the training ground for the Knights of the Round Table.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I go in there?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Of course, my lord. But I must tell you the rules first. Would you like to hear them now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: The training ground is a melee combat area only. Merlin has cast a spell over the grounds to prevent anyone using Prayer, Ranged or Magic.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Upon entering the grounds, you will fight each of the Round Table Knights in turn. Each knight is more skilled than the last, but all have different attack styles that can be exploited. There are eight knights in all, and

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: each will do damage not only to your hitpoints, but also your combat stats. If you should die in the grounds, Merlin will teleport you back here. You will not lose any of your items.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: If you feel you cannot complete the battle, you may exit the training grounds. However, if you exit or die, you will have to start over with the first knight the next time you enter.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: If you are a great warrior and defeat Sir Lancelot, mightiest of all knights, you will receive great rewards. Two new prayers will become available to you and your respawn point will be set to Camelot, as well as 20,000

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: XP in to each of your combat stats.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: If you do not want your respawn set to Camelot, Merlin can change it for you.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Would you like me to repeat any of that for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the training ground.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Only melee combat is allowed within the training grounds. Merlin will prevent the use of Prayer, Ranged and Magic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: These grounds are for only the best knights of the land. It is not just testing your combat ability, but your honour and your chivalry. You must face your enemy head on, honourably and bravely.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Be watchful of your stats, as the Knights can do damage to your combat stats as well as your hitpoints.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about dying or leaving the grounds.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: As these are only training grounds, we keep our knights safe. If you should die, you will be teleported back to here and all of your items will be safe.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: However, leaving the training grounds, in anyway, will mean you will have to start from the beginning the next time you enter. These grounds do not favour cowards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have to start again? What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: You fight each knight of the Round Table, starting with Sir Bedivere and finishing with Sir Lancelot. Leaving means you will have to begin fighting Sir Bedivere again and work your way back up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if I log out during the fights?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: If you log out during a fight, upon logging back in you will fight the knight you were fighting when you logged out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the rewards.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: King Arthur believes in rewarding his knights for their chivalry. Having proven yourself in combat against his knights, and in your services rendered to Camelot, you will be rewarded with two new prayers, and a new

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: respawn at Camelot, as well as 20,000 XP in to each of your combat stats.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do the new prayers do?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Ah, patience is a virtue in knights, my lord. Fight in the training ground and earn your reward to find out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then tell me about the respawn - how will it work?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Ah, Merlin has offered, as a reward for your help, to let your respawn in the safety of Camelot, should you befall misfortune elsewhere in the world. Should you wish to change your respawn back to Lumbridge, simply

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: speak to Merlin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, thank you.

sections.title: Talking to Squire again after having granted an access

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Good day, my lord. Is there anything I can do for you?

sections.title: Trying to enter the Training Grounds with a follower

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Well done, young adventurer. You truly are a worthy knight.

sections.title: Talking to Merlin near the training grounds

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Excuse me for rushing off like this, but I must get back to my workroom.
